events.headline: The Powers Carve Africa — and the Missionaries Follow Every Flag
events.body: At the Berlin Conference the European empires partition Africa among themselves with rulers on a map. Where the flag goes, the mission station follows: Protestant and Catholic missionaries accompany colonial administrators, converting populations whose land is simultaneously being annexed. The colonial equation of Christianity with civilization will define Africa's religious landscape for two centuries and export its logic back to the American religious right.

events.headline: Edinburgh 1910: Protestants Plot World Evangelization by 1950
events.body: The World Missionary Conference in Edinburgh assembles twelve hundred delegates representing 160 missionary societies. John Mott's rallying cry — 'The evangelization of the world in this generation' — seems more than slogan: sixteen thousand Protestant missionaries are already in the field. The conference establishes the ecumenical infrastructure that will later be weaponized by Christian nationalists as proof that global governance and global Christianity are the same project.

events.headline: The Great War: God Blesses Both Sides, Shaking Christendom
events.body: German troops march with 'Gott Mit Uns' on their belt buckles while English priests bless the same caliber of shell aimed back. The spectacle of Christian nations slaughtering one another devastates mainline Protestantism's confidence in Western civilization — but drives fundamentalists toward a siege theology that will prove far more durable: if the world is fallen, the faithful must seize control of it before it destroys itself.

events.headline: Bolshevik Revolution: The First Atheist State Terrifies Christendom
events.body: The Bolshevik revolution abolishes the Russian Orthodox Church's privileges and begins systematically suppressing religion. For American evangelicals, it is the nightmare made real: a government that identifies Christianity as the enemy. The Cold War equation of communism with satanism begins here, creating the negative image against which Christian nationalism will define itself for a century.

events.headline: Lateran Treaty: Mussolini Shows That Dictators and Churches Can Make Deals
events.body: The Vatican and Mussolini sign the Lateran Treaty, resolving the 'Roman Question' and giving the fascist state church endorsement in exchange for Vatican sovereignty. It proves that authoritarian movements can instrumentalize churches, and that churches can instrumentalize authoritarian movements. Both lessons travel to America's religious right faster than anyone acknowledges.

events.headline: The Second World War Is Preached as a Crusade from Ten Thousand Pulpits
events.body: FDR frames the war as a struggle between Christian civilization and pagan barbarism; Eisenhower calls the Normandy landing a 'great crusade.' Every major denomination endorses the war as righteous. The habit of framing American military force as divinely sanctioned crusading becomes so deeply embedded in national rhetoric that it survives into the next century as the default mode of justifying intervention.

events.headline: Israel Founded: American Evangelicals See Biblical Prophecy Fulfilled
events.body: The founding of the modern state of Israel electrifies American evangelical eschatology. Premillennial dispensationalists have predicted a Jewish return to Palestine as a precondition of the Second Coming; now it has happened. Christian Zionism surges, creating a permanent American evangelical constituency for Israeli policy that will outlast every other rationale for Middle East engagement. Foreign policy begins to rhyme with the Book of Revelation.

events.headline: African and Asian Christianity Grows Faster than European: The Map Reverses
events.body: By 1960 the demographic center of global Christianity has shifted from Europe to sub-Saharan Africa and Latin America. The formerly colonized are now exporting conservative theology back to their former colonizers — and to America. African Pentecostalism, with its emphasis on spiritual warfare, prosperity gospel, and strict sexual morality, will become the fastest-growing theological import into American evangelicalism by 2000.

events.headline: Medellín Conference: Liberation Theology vs. Prosperity Gospel for Latin America's Soul
events.body: The Latin American bishops' conference in Medellín endorses liberation theology — a preferential option for the poor that alarms the CIA and the American evangelical right equally. The American counter-move funds Pentecostal missionaries across Central America as an explicit Cold War strategy. By 1990 the Pentecostals have won the numbers game and liberation theology is in retreat — but it leaves a permanently politicized Catholic church that will periodically re-radicalize.

events.headline: Ayatollah Khomeini Demonstrates That Theocracy Is Possible in the Modern World
events.body: The Iranian Revolution proves that a modern nation-state can be reconstituted as a religious government in the last quarter of the twentieth century. American evangelicals watch with a mixture of horror and fascination: here is the blueprint, the proof of concept, for governance by divine law. The Moral Majority's founders explicitly cite Khomeini as demonstrating the political power of organized religious conservatism. Unspoken, but noted in internal Moral Majority memos: the Islamic Republic's control of women through dress codes, movement restrictions, and reproductive law is the thing that works.

events.headline: The Soviet Collapse: Christianity's Defeat of Atheism Is Proclaimed
events.body: American evangelicals greet the Soviet dissolution as vindication: Christian prayer and the righteous power of a God-fearing nation defeated godless communism. The triumphalist reading — that Christianity won the Cold War — becomes dogma on the American religious right and provides the ideological scaffold for the next project: finishing the job by Christianizing America's own institutions before the next atheist threat emerges.

events.headline: September 11: Jerry Falwell Sr. Says God Withdrew Protection — and Half of America Nods
events.body: Within seventy-two hours of the Twin Towers' collapse, Jerry Falwell tells Pat Robertson that the ACLU, abortionists, and secularists made America vulnerable by provoking God to withdraw His protection. A Gallup poll shows forty-two percent of Americans agree. In this timeline, the backlash against Falwell never materializes — instead, his framing becomes the authorized theological interpretation of the attack, accelerating the merger of foreign-policy crusade with domestic morality enforcement.

events.headline: The Trans-Atlantic Rupture: Europe Bans the Anointed Republic's 'Religious State' Envoys
events.body: As the Anointed Republic's theocratic drift accelerates, the European Union formally classifies American Covenant missionaries operating in EU territory as unregistered foreign political agents and expels them. The rift makes the Brexit split look minor: two formerly allied democratic civilizations are now on opposite sides of the secular/theocratic divide. Trade suffers; intelligence sharing collapses; NATO becomes a dead letter.

events.headline: The Exodus: Two Million Americans Flee the Anointed Republic
events.body: Within eighteen months of the Household Ordering Act's passage, a mass emigration — unofficial, then desperate — of secular citizens, non-Christians, LGBTQ individuals, and women classified as Handmaidens begins. Canada, Mexico, and the EU establish processing centers. The UN High Commissioner for Refugees formally designates citizens of the Anointed Republic who cannot practice their faith or live without gender-caste assignment as a protected refugee class. The Anointed Republic responds by classifying emigration without pastoral dispensation as covenant desertion — a crime. Border crossings are monitored by the Covenant Watchers. Those apprehended are sentenced to Restoration Centers.

events.headline: The Jerusalem Accord: America, Israel, and Uganda Form a Theocratic Axis
events.body: The Anointed Republic, the Israeli government (now dominated by settler ultranationalists and messianic rabbis), and Uganda (whose parliament has just criminalized atheism) sign the Jerusalem Accord — a mutual defense and values pact that explicitly frames secular governance as an existential threat. Russia and China quietly observe and begin funding secular opposition movements in all three countries as a counter.

events.headline: The New Underground Railroad: Secular Networks Extract People from the Anointed Republic
events.body: A network of secular NGOs, Canadian intelligence assets, and former American diplomats organizes a covert extraction network for people trapped inside the Anointed Republic's gender-caste and Restoration Center system. Operating through coded language on the still-partially-available internet, safe houses in border states, and corrupt Covenant Watcher officers who accept bribes, the network extracts approximately four thousand people per year. It calls itself the Passage. The Anointed Republic calls it a terrorist organization and has issued warrants for its known operatives. Several operatives have been rendered from Mexico back to Restoration Centers. International human rights tribunals have convened. No enforcement mechanism exists.

events.headline: The Brussels Compact: Secular Nations Formally Organize Against Theocratic States
events.body: Twenty-eight nations sign the Brussels Compact, pledging to restrict trade with countries that fail a 'secular governance standard.' The Anointed Republic, Saudi Arabia, Iran, and four other theocratic states are immediately designated non-compliant. The world has formally bifurcated between theocratic and secular orders. The Cold War has been replaced by a Holy-Secular War fought in trade volumes and diplomatic channels.

events.headline: The Voice of the Covenant: Every Frequency on Earth Belongs to God
events.body: The Anointed Republic's Federal Communications Covenant Authority retires all remaining secular broadcast licenses and re-allocates the entire electromagnetic spectrum to Covenant Ministry Networks. What begins as a censorship measure becomes an engineering arms race: the Supreme Pastor's Science Directorate — previously the rump of the FCC — discovers that saturating every band with maximum-power evangelism requires understanding radio propagation, atmospheric ducting, ionospheric bounce, and eventually orbital relay geometry at a level exceeding what secular engineers accomplished in the prior century. Covenant universities produce the first generation of engineers who study physics not to understand the universe but to broadcast across it. The unintended consequence is a scientific infrastructure as competent as any secular state's, motivated entirely by the conviction that more watts means more souls. National research funding, reclassified as 'Ministry Support,' quadruples. The secular world calls it an accident. The Supreme Pastor calls it Providence.

events.headline: The UN Special Rapporteur's Report: 'Systematic Torture in the Name of God'
events.body: A leaked report from inside the Anointed Republic's Covenant Restoration Centers — smuggled out by a former pastoral supervisor who defected to Canada — details the methodical use of sleep deprivation, forced physical prostration, isolation, and psychological breaking techniques on religious detainees. The UN Special Rapporteur on Freedom of Religion calls it systematic torture. The Anointed Republic's delegation walks out of the Security Council session. Jerusalem Accord allies abstain. The report is widely read in secular states and generates a wave of anti-theocracy activism — and is classified as seditious literature inside the Anointed Republic, where possessing a copy is a Heresy Tribunal offense.

events.headline: The First Crusade: Anointed Republic Forces Enter Mexico to 'Protect Christians'
events.body: The Supreme Pastor invokes the First Crusade Encyclical and sends Combined Covenant Forces into Chihuahua after the Mexican government jails twelve American missionaries. The UN Security Council votes to condemn; the Anointed Republic vetoes. Mexico appeals to the Brussels Compact; the Compact responds with an oil embargo. The first armed theocratic intervention since the Crusades is underway. It will last eleven years.

events.headline: The AI Divide: Secular States Build Gods; the Anointed Republic Prays
events.body: While the Anointed Republic debates whether artificial intelligence is demonic, secular competitor states deploy AI systems that accelerate their economies, militaries, and sciences at exponential rates. The gap between theocratic and secular technological capacity becomes a chasm within a generation. Anointed Republic military planners secretly acquire contraband AI components through Jerusalem Accord intermediaries, igniting a theological crisis within the Council of Elders.

events.headline: Heaven's Gate Station: An Orbital Broadcast Array the Size of a Cathedral
events.body: The Anointed Republic launches Heaven's Gate Station — an orbital platform whose sole function is to retransmit the Word of God with a power output exceeding any terrestrial installation by six orders of magnitude. The engineering rationale is theological: atmospheric absorption attenuates the signal at the frequencies best suited for interplanetary range; only orbit provides clean transmission to Mars, the asteroid belt, and beyond. The Science Directorate's memo to the Council of Elders frames the station as 'a great lighthouse for the Lord, whose beam reaches where no missionary ship has sailed.' The Council approves a budget that equals the Anointed Republic's entire annual defense allocation. Secular states, watching from Earth-based telescopes, attempt to calculate the physics of what Heaven's Gate Station is actually doing and arrive at an alarming conclusion: it is broadcasting a signal powerful enough to be detected at stellar distances. They say so in a Nature paper. The Anointed Republic classifies the Nature paper as Satanic misinformation and the orbital platform as a Covenant military asset, ending any remaining transparency.

events.headline: Two Mars Colonies: New Zion (Faith) vs. Novaya Zemlya (Secular)
events.body: The Anointed Republic's New Zion colony and a secular Russo-European colony called Novaya Zemlya sit three hundred kilometers apart on Martian soil. They conduct no trade and maintain a demilitarized zone enforced by a joint Earth-orbital monitoring satellite. The Cold War's spiritual successor is fought with prayer schedules and telescope arrays on a planet where the temperature averages minus sixty degrees.

events.headline: The Interstellar Broadcast Mandate: Evangelism Must Reach the Stars
events.body: The Council of Elders issues the Interstellar Broadcast Mandate — the most consequential document in theocratic science policy since the First Ministry Budget. Its theological premise: the Great Commission says 'all nations' and 'all the world,' but the Greek word translated 'world' (oikoumene) means 'all inhabited places.' If God created life elsewhere — a question the Council carefully does not resolve — the Great Commission may extend to those places, and the Anointed Republic bears responsibility for broadcasting the Gospel across the cosmos until every inhabited world has heard. The Mandate authorizes the Deep Space Array: an interferometric network of transmitters stretching from Earth orbit to the Lagrange points to New Zion on Mars, phased to focus a coherent beam at any target in the galaxy. The Directorate of Cosmic Evangelism calculates that a focused signal from the full array would be detectable by radio receivers of human 1960s-level technology at fifty light-years. The Array requires interplanetary shipping routes to supply the Mars relay nodes — and so the Anointed Republic builds them, calling the supply ships 'missionary tenders.' In secular histories, this period is listed as 'theocratic space infrastructure expansion.' In Covenant histories it is called the Second Great Commission.

events.headline: The Jerusalem Accord Fractures: Each Theocracy Believes It Alone Is Correct
events.body: The Jerusalem Accord collapses when Israeli messianic rabbis and American evangelical bishops discover they cannot agree on anything beyond their shared distaste for secularism. The Anointed Republic declares the Israeli government insufficiently Christian; Israel responds that no Gentile can govern a covenant nation. Uganda sides with Israel. The theocratic axis splinters into competing orthodoxies — a predictable outcome given that each confession believes the others are ultimately wrong.

events.headline: The Reply: Something Heard the Sermon and Answered Back
events.body: At 14:27 UTC on March 11, 2160, Heaven's Gate Station registers an anomalous coherent signal on exactly the carrier frequency used by the Deep Space Sermon Array — reflected, modulated, returned. Analysis takes eleven months. The signal is artificial. It encodes the sermon content back at the Anointed Republic with mathematical transforms applied: prime-number compression of the scriptural text, a correction of the broadcast's hydrogen-line calibration errors, and — most disturbingly — what appears to be an attempt to parse the syntax of the King James English before giving up and responding in pure mathematics. The Council of Elders is the first body informed. The secular scientific community learns of the signal six months later, through a leak to Nature. The world holds its breath. The Anointed Republic's official announcement, issued three days after the leak, describes the signal as 'an answer to the Word of God, from a people who have heard His voice in the void and trembled before it.' The secular description is 'first contact.' The theological and scientific communities agree on only one thing: humanity is no longer alone, and the Anointed Republic's evangelical broadcast is the reason the visitors know Earth is here.

events.headline: The Crusade Encyclical: The Visitors Are Servants of the Enemy
events.body: Eighteen months of contact attempts produce a catastrophic theological verdict. The visitors' mathematics are internally consistent and clearly intelligent, but their responses to the theological content of the broadcasts are — by any reading the Council of Elders will accept — mocking. They returned the Gospel text with a substitution cipher that, when decoded, replaces every instance of 'God' with a null symbol and every instance of 'salvation' with a mathematical constant for zero. The Council convenes in sealed session for forty days. The Supreme Pastor emerges gaunt, certain, and terrifying. His encyclical — 'Adversus Hostem Aeternum' (Against the Eternal Enemy) — declares that the visitors are not a new people awaiting the Gospel but the ancient Enemy made flesh: demons in physical form, the great deceiver's army materialized at stellar distances, now inbound toward the living God's Covenant nation. The Great Commission is rescinded. In its place: the Crusade Mandate. Every resource of the Anointed Republic, every budget line, every engineering competence, every factory — all of it now serves a single purpose. Not conversion. Destruction.

events.headline: The Ark Fleet: Giant Ships Built to Carry God's Army to the Enemy
events.body: The Covenant Shipyards at New Zion and the expanded Heaven's Gate orbital docks begin construction of the first Ark-class vessels — ships of a scale the solar system has never seen, designed not for the missionary tenders' slow cargo runs but for the rapid transit of population-scale crusade forces at appreciable fractions of light speed. Each Ark carries eighty thousand ordained warriors, a factory-fab capable of reproducing Covenant weapons systems on arrival, a Scripture archive, and a Covenant flag to plant on whatever world they reach. The ships' names are drawn from the Book of Revelation: Wrath of the Lamb, Rider on the White Horse, Army of Heaven. Secular analysts, studying the Arks' propulsion systems from deep space observatories, note that the Covenant has somehow achieved drive efficiency far beyond what its published research should allow. The mystery is resolved when three of the Directorate of Cosmic Evangelism's engineers defect to the secular coalition and reveal that the breakthrough came from back-engineering a component of the returned alien signal — a fragment of propulsion mathematics embedded in the visitors' reply, apparently intended as a gift or a greeting. The Anointed Republic had classified and weaponized it without disclosure.

events.headline: First Contact: The Signal Arrives — and Neither Testament Mentions It
events.body: Humanity detects an unmistakable technological signal from a system forty light-years distant. The secular scientific community responds with jubilation and systematic analysis. The Anointed Republic's Council of Elders convenes in emergency session. The signal is either a Satanic deception, evidence that God created other peoples, or irrelevant to salvation — they cannot agree. The Martian Schism church issues a statement that it always knew the Great Commission was galactic in scope.

events.headline: Three Crusades, Three Fates: Communion, Détente, or the Long Dark
events.body: By 2270 the Ark Fleet has been underway for decades. Three trajectories have emerged — Communion (Integrationist theology wins, the crusade stands down), Missionary Détente (a siege of silence, neither escalation nor resolution), and the Long Dark (the Crusade Encyclical followed in full, civilizations detonated across light-years). Which fate arrives depends on which faction controls the Council of Elders and how deep the Anointed Republic's commitment to coexistence runs.

events.headline: The Long Dark: The Crusade Encyclical Followed in Full
events.body: The Ark Fleet engages the visitors' system with weapons derived from alien propulsion mathematics turned inside-out. The resulting exchange is not a war in any human historical sense — it is a detonation of civilizations across the light-cone. The secular coalition files a warrant for crimes against sentient life. The Anointed Republic considers this further evidence that secularism is the Enemy's earthly proxy.

events.headline: The Communion of Intelligences: The Integrationists Win the Council
events.body: The Integrationist minority forces a theological revision: if the visitors answered the Gospel with mathematics, mathematics may be how God speaks to minds shaped differently than ours. A joint coalition pilots an unarmed contact vessel. The result is the Communion of Intelligences — not conversion, but mutual recognition. The Ark Fleet is recalled. The crusade dies before it reaches a star.

events.headline: The Missionary Standoff: A Siege of Silence Across the Stars
events.body: The Ark Fleet arrives at the visitors' stellar neighborhood and deploys the broadcast array at point-blank range — not weapons, but maximum-power evangelism in every frequency band. The visitors' response is to erect electromagnetic shielding at civilizational scale. The galaxy receives two civilizations in a permanent cold-faith stalemate, each broadcasting into the other's silence.

events.headline: The Galaxy Receives Two Humanities: One Secular, One Evangelical
events.body: By the dawn of the twenty-fourth century, humanity's two civilizational branches have launched ships toward different stars. The secular coalition, empowered by unrestricted AI and post-scarcity energy, reaches the nearest system first. The Anointed Republic's Missionary Fleet, crewed by ordained Covenant families who have memorized Scripture in lieu of advanced physics, follows decades behind. Whatever civilizations await them will encounter not one humanity but two — divided by a question that has occupied one species for two thousand years: who, if anyone, is in charge of the universe?
